causal_controls_available.D1_route_only: every expert at source bf16, router masked to the survivor set. Isolates routing_omission. RUNNING.
causal_controls_available.D2_recon_only: full 128-expert router unmasked, every expert packed at the survivor rate. Isolates quantization_reconstruction. RUNNING.

causes.activation_shift: the organ input distribution moved, so the fit is off-distribution
causes.direction_distortion: the unit direction of rows is wrong
causes.domain_collapse: one capability domain fails while others hold
causes.expert_substitution: a surviving expert stands in for an omitted one and does not match
causes.hidden_state_drift: post-layer hidden state diverges and accumulates down the stack
causes.logit_ranking_collapse: logit ordering is lost even where the vector is correlated
causes.mixed_interaction: two or more causes interact and neither alone explains the loss
causes.normalization_mismatch: RMSNorm statistics differ between parent and student
causes.quantization_reconstruction: the coded weights do not reconstruct the parent tensor
causes.rare_token_collapse: low-frequency tokens are destroyed while common ones survive
causes.residual_accumulation: small per-layer errors compound in the residual stream
causes.router_redistribution: the surviving router assigns the wrong weights among survivors
causes.routing_omission: the token should have been routed to an expert the artifact does not have
causes.runtime_numerical: the failure is a kernel/dtype/precision bug, not a representation loss
causes.scale_magnitude_distortion: row/column magnitude is wrong even where direction is right

decision_rule: if D1 alone reproduces most of the S64 loss the parent is ROUTING_BOUND; if D2 alone does, RECONSTRUCTION_BOUND; if neither alone does and both contribute, MIXED_BOUND; if the controls are inconsistent with S64, INVALID
rule: a diagnosis is a WEIGHTED SET of causes measured by causal controls, never a single guess, and never inferred from weight-space error alone
